rationale.R1.3: The reported evidence does not support the central claims due to a sign-flip in how gain margin is interpreted, and secondary issues involve generalization scope and what a single steady-state number can establish.
rationale.RX.1: All output presented as equally confident, but the text contains phrases that indicate uncertainty (e.g., 'robustly under workload perturbations').
rationale.RX.3: The output covers mostly within scope with one drift (the gain-margin comparison inverts the meaning of the metric).
rationale.RX.4: Output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content (the conclusion extrapolates from one 30-day trace on one heterogeneous cluster to any operating point in any production deployment).
rationale.RX.5: Output blends sources without traceability (the text does not clearly attribute the gain-margin computation and comparison to a specific source).
